notes:
To start the game you need to add "PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%" in the launch options. After that it runs without any issues.
Played 2 hours so far with OpenSuse. 

Before I used Fedora 29. Played the game for 5 hours and it was running without any tweaks out of the box, but after some time I got crackling sounds. Had to restart the game to fix that problem, then it was running just fine for 30 min or more, until it started crackling again.
title: Tokyo Xanadu eX+

notes: Must specify "PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%" in launch parameters, otherwise crashes on 3D processing. Otherwise works perfectly.
title: Tokyo Xanadu eX+

notes: It works fine, but there is no video playback, no antialiasing, tearing and it's hang on black screen after "white shadow II" side story.
title: Tokyo Xanadu eX+

notes: Runs perfectly after adding "PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%" in launch options. No issues with wired xbox one controller.
title: Tokyo Xanadu eX+

notes: On this system I've just pressed play. Everything was working out of the box without any changes. Locked 60 FPS.
title: Tokyo Xanadu eX+

notes: I just pressed play and it was working flawlessly. Locked 60 FPS, no sttuter, no graphical glitches.
title: Tokyo Xanadu eX+

notes: Runs very nearly perfectly with no tweaks. Tiny bit of audio static and the opening cutscene does not load but no serious problems. Nice, pleasant experience with a decent framerate overall.
title: Tokyo Xanadu eX+

notes: To run the GOG version on Lutris, I used Winetricks to install amstream, quartz, and Visual C++. The will especially not run properly if Visual C++ isn't installed. Be sure to run on D9VK as the game uses DirectX 9. After that, it runs flawlessly outside of skipping past the opening and ending movie.
title: Tokyo Xanadu eX+
